reviews.title: I would not recommend staying there
reviews.positives: Nothing.
reviews.negatives: The area was not very welcoming.   The building and reception area looked good.  But that was all..  One elevator was out of order.  The place had a bad odor.  The bathroom sink had ants. The cost of the room was definitely not worth what was paid.

reviews.title: Convenient but Terrible
reviews.text: The week at this hotel had me wishing I would return back to home in the US. The location is quite nice, and some of the staff members are great. However, there's a lot wrong. No hot shower water. Old hotel and smell in rooms. Air conditioning did not work well and it was very hot. Overall I will be happy to pay more for a better hotel and never return here.

reviews.title: Would NEVER recommend this hotel!
reviews.negatives: Mold in the shower, terrible towels, shabby interiors

reviews.title: Only Location
reviews.text:
Location is amazing.
But you have to give up cleanliness.
Towels are very old and after you wipe your face with it, some of dusts or something are sticking on your face.
Don’t look down below the bed.
Room is quite big but not useful.

reviews.title: Excellent location!
reviews.text:
+ Excellent location (don't go there by car, use the amazing and cheapest of all Orlando parcheggio), great view from the top. Large rooms, good breakfast (especially the sweets), the best coffee is on the second page of the menu on the machines. Professional and nice staff.
- Bad soundproofing, quite empty rooms in need of some maintenance. The bathroom smells a bit of sewage and there's a really strong perfume in the corridors.

reviews.title: Location One Tool Accommodation
reviews.text:
The location and rooftop terrace are really excellent. However, you have to eat with pigeons during breakfast (the same goes for indoors).

The bathroom drain is clogged, so the water doesn't go down, and there is a constant smell of excrement, making it damp all day. There is an air conditioner, but it's only as good as a fan, and there are no amenities at all.

The location is really nice. Some of the staff are very friendly, but most cannot speak English.

reviews.title: Good
reviews.text: No in room kettle, free water or instant coffee ☹️

reviews.title: This place is a good location, but a risk to stay in. I probably just had bad luck with the bed bugs
reviews.positives: Good location, that’s all
reviews.negatives: I think my room had bed bugs! The next morning I woke up with over 20 bites on my arms. The room didn’t feel very clean and it smelled so strongly of perfumed spray that maybe they used to mask not cleaning properly.

reviews.title: B&B Hotel Palermo Quattro Canti reviews
reviews.text:
1. Pros
  - It's located in the city center, so there are many good restaurants around.
  - The staff is extremely friendly.

2. Cons
  - The room (especially the bathtub) was not clean.
  - It's noisy at night because it’s a busy area.

I recommend it for those who prioritize location, but I don’t recommend it for those who value cleanliness and soundproofing. I had a good experience with the location and low price.

reviews.title: Not good if you want to sleep after midnight.
reviews.positives: Staff and cleanliness
reviews.negatives: Loud music until 2Am.  It forced us to check out early and loss the money spent for day two

reviews.title: Nice but very noisy
reviews.text: The hotel overall could be pleasant and in a central location, but you will not be able to sleep with the loud music until well past midnight, as well as the local chattering etc. amenities are poor, staff is very nice and helpful and the hotel is overall clean.

reviews.title: Very good
reviews.text: good location, nice roof terace, ok room, below average breakfast

reviews.title: Acceptable
reviews.positives: Great location and breakfast
reviews.negatives: Checked in at 1pm. Air Cond did not work. Room temp 27C. Alerted front desk of problem. Returned to hotel at 6pm. Nothing was done. Was offered another room. Aic cond did not work in that room either. Stayed in original room without AC and had a miserable night. Next day we were offered another room. Smaller and below what we had paid for.

reviews.title: Depends on your neighbor
reviews.text: Very well placed and clean hotel with friendly staff, but the soundproofing is awful, especially when your neighbor takes a shower

reviews.title: It was a problematic two-star hotel in a great location.
reviews.positives: Location was superb.  You can walk to all the major churches and museums without difficulty.
reviews.negatives:
Everything could have been improved on.

We knew going in that the street facing the hotel could be noisy until the wee hours. I am very hard of hearing, yet even so, the partying outside (weeknights!) kept me awake. As the noise was far worse than expected, request an interior room.

Nice outdoor terrace on 5th floor, but subtly under-maintained, with unhealthy plants and need of sprucing up. Breakfast was less than pictured in ads, with less fruit on offer. Good bakery selection. Coffee machines sometimes on blink.  Scrambled eggs always room temperature, even when brought out fresh from kitchen. 

Room (avoid 312, as it, faces the street and is across from elevator) was full of mop debris and little dust bunnies. Furniture was worn and scuffed.  Nasty foam pillows.  No extra blanket.  Most disturbing: bathroom was without hot water most of the day and evening, with mold in the grouting and no amenities but a big bottle of bath wash, as you buy everything else downstairs.

For those staying during warmer weather, the A/C isn’t turned on until May 1.

Some staff were both friendly and helpful. Some were bored and dismissive.  All had good English.

reviews.title: City center hotel
reviews.text: good hotel location , room size is good

reviews.title: Most noisy hotel ever. Never again.
reviews.positives: great location, ok breakfast
reviews.negatives: It is the most noisy hotel I've even been to. On the first morning constructions started at 7am, with metal drilling waking everyone up. We were on the top floor, above us the restaurant, where they were moving all furnitures all evening long until 10pm. Then again starting 7am the noise from moving things around for breakfast woke everyone up. There are no carpets anywhere and the staff is loud in the hallways as well. Basically one can only sleep between 10pm and 7am. Never again.

reviews.title: Nice staff but poorly advertised
reviews.text: Room was advertised as a city view… instead we had a view of the outside of the lift shaft. Breakfast was also poor.

reviews.title: Great location
reviews.text: It is very close to Quattro Canti, a landmark in the old town. Most attractions, including the Mercato Ballaro, Mercato Vucciria, the Cathedral, etc are near.  It's about a 15-20min walk (with my luggage) to Parlemo Centrale (main train station). The room was cleaned daily. However, I find the room was rather dim. I read in my room and there was no light above the writing table. I also discovered that Parlemo is quite unlike the other 8 cities of Italy that I have visited. In fact, it feels like a different country to me.

reviews.title: Hotel located in the city center.
reviews.text: The hotel is about a 10-15 minute walk from the train station. The bus stop to Tripani is about a 7-minute walk away. However, the hotel is located right in the middle of downtown, so when you step outside, the main intersection, restaurants, and a church are all nearby. The rooftop lounge bar on the 5th floor offers a direct view of the church, and it seems quite popular as they frequently host parties. During the weekend, there was a salsa party one day and another party the next day. However, the interior of the hotel has a maze-like corridor, and the condition of the bed, bathroom, and view are not great. The room was spacious.

reviews.title: Clean, basic, beat up
reviews.text: The room was clean but felt beat up. Even though it was clean, there was something wcheevy about the room. The bigger problem was that there was not adequate security. The room door only had a self locking handle. It didn't have any other additional lock or a security chain. I do not recommend this for female travellers. Although the room didn't smell, the public areas had a really weird smell to them, not sure if it was mold. Nonetheless, it smelt really bad. The location was great, but very noisy and the staff were really nice.

reviews.title: Great stay
reviews.text: PrimeLocation nice and clean perfect for a short stay

reviews.title: Not worth the price!!!
reviews.positives: Location
reviews.negatives: Safety and sanitary/hygiene concerns . Mold/mildew the shower was disgusting. I actually messaged the property about all my concerns.

reviews.title: Great location, good hotel, but beware the plumbing
reviews.text: Great location, good price, great staff. Only problem was the sewerage smell when it rained. Staff handled the problem well with a replacement room and upgrade, but the first night with the smell in the room was awful.

reviews.title: Not advisable.
reviews.positives: Location convenience in old town
reviews.negatives: Noisy. Construction noise starting at 8:00 am every morning was unbearable. Lack of carpet throughout the hotel increased the inside noise coming from other rooms and the hallway. The air condition unit was not clean to have a healthy air circulation (see the photo).

reviews.title: A basic room in a good location
reviews.text: This hotel is pretty basic but in a central location to restaurants, the Cathedral and Norman Palace. The room did not look like the picture. The shower door does not open very wide as it’s beside the toilet. I had a room that faced the inside so no real window. The breakfast was simple with yogurt, meats and croissants but I think you are better off eating out. There are cafes right across the street. I wasn’t expecting an amazing hotel room so it was ok. Lots of families were staying here.  If you want a budget hotel this is a good place.

reviews.title: Great location, staff helpful, very sad hotel.
reviews.positives: The location is great. Breakfast area is very nice on top floor with a view.
reviews.negatives: Very worn scruffy rooms. First room looked into a utility shaft and party voices echoed loudly. I requested another room which they quickly agreed to. 2nd room: loud noises above from big party above, live band and then clean up (tables scraping along the floor) kept us awake until after 1am when I went up and complained.

reviews.title: Good value for money
reviews.text: The hotel itself is a little outdated and unfortunately doesn’t really look like the pictures anymore. When booking we were also promised a ‘city view’. There was in fact no view. Just a wall behind our window which made me feel very claustrophobic . However, it is located literally in the center of Palermo, which almost makes up for the above. The staff were pleasant and helpful. All in all good value for money.

reviews.title: Very good
reviews.text: Hotel is really well placed for central palermo. Only 15 mins to walk to the train station. Rooftop bar views are fantastic and a real added bonus. Reception staff were polite and their English was excellent. Good options for eating and drinking all around the hotel.

reviews.title: Decent hotel
reviews.text: Decent accommodations, central location close to attractions. building needs a bit of TLC/renovations, but very clean

reviews.title: Read this before booking.
reviews.positives: Cuisine in rooftop restaurant very good (but see below). Room is large and comfortable.
reviews.negatives: Hotel offers it's guests 15%off food at its roof top restaurant which is managed by a separate company. The other company's employee handling payment of our dinner bill did not apply the discount to our bill. We tried to get her to correct her mistake but she refused. We tried to get Frnt Desk to help, she still refused. But she offered free drinks to compensate, which means she knew she was wrong. So, be.advised.

reviews.negatives: The noise. Also, my room, though clean, was a bit shabby.

reviews.title: It was ok for the short stay but could have been better.

reviews.negatives: It was difficult to get to as a foreigner via GPS. The space could have been a bit more cleaner. There was a smell in the lobby that wasn’t pleasant for my sinus. I borrowed a kettle and it was very dirty.

reviews.title: Excellent location
reviews.text:
Here are what stand out, apart from being good value:
Location: literarily just steps away from the historic City Center. close to the train station, too.
Rooftop: nice view and a Lively vibe.

reviews.title: Acceptable
reviews.positives: Location ideal

reviews.title: Central Palermo, no frills
reviews.text:
Unbeatable location with train station and most of historical centre within 15 minutes walk. Large property with newer rooms in an annex, accessed through several corridors on different levels.

Communication could be improved with pre-arrival email and messages going unanswered. Housekeeping seemed confused over towel change request and do not disturb signage.  Issues quickly addressed by front office though avoidable.

Room amenities were adequate though basic. Towels and liquid soap dispersers for example were somewhat worn.  Without better maintenance, value only at discounted rates.

reviews.title: Acceptable
reviews.positives: The hotel is located in the old historical district which is great for sightseeing and walking around. Great coffee and ice cream around the corner on Via Maqueda at Ruvolo
reviews.negatives:
The staff could be nicer. It seemed like we were bothering them just by checking in. The  hotel is located within a limited traffic area so you need a permit to drive to the hotel. We didn’t know this and the hotel told us they’d give us a permit when we checked but they did not mention they would only give us for 2 days and we’d have to buy our own. They told us this 2 days later after we used the 2 day permit.

The hotel isn’t accessible for wheelchairs/strollers. One of the gentleman at front desk was nice enough to offer us to use the side emergency exit that was wheelchair/stroller accessible.

The room’s definitely do not look like in the pictures… very dated and could use some paint and decor. The room we were given the ac was at 16 the whole time and it was still soo hot in the room. I told front desk about this but no one came to check…

The “rooftop” is just the top floor of the hotel. Nothing special.

Breakfast was a mainly pastries.

reviews.title: Excellent hotel
reviews.text: Loves the roof terrace for breakfast and drinks. Great location with so many sights walkable

reviews.title: Anyway if I will return in Palermo I will return to this hotel

reviews.negatives: Bathroom  large  but old and not in good condition

reviews.title: Best value for money, best location, and best staff, especially Valeria!
reviews.text:
Located right in the center of Palermo, in front of the main square, the building has a café and restaurant on the roof where you can enjoy the night view. The building is old, but the prices are incredibly cheap. Best value for money! The hotel’s coin laundry was challenging to use since the language was Italian, but an employee named Valeria always greeted me with a smile and was very kind, allowing me to do my laundry without any issues. 
The neighborhood is very beautiful, and each building is historical. In the evening, an opera trainee(?!) sings for free, making the neighborhood feel like an opera house. 
There’s also a small market nearby where an elderly fruit vendor noticed the unzipped zipper of my bag while I was taking out my wallet, smiled, and gestured for me to keep it closed. He gave me an absurd amount of mini tomatoes for just 1 euro. Yet, their aroma was art. I want to go again.

reviews.title: You need to make reservations properly.
reviews.text:
If you make a reservation for the side that says "Courtyard View," you may only see a wall or have no view at all, leading to a life without natural light. I strongly recommend booking a room by the window.

The reason I'm leaving a review is to remember later. I've visited twice, and each time I had to stay in a dim room.

On the positive side, there was a small refrigerator and a bathtub, but due to the lack of natural light, it was a downside that there was a musty smell constantly in the accommodation.

However, it might be hard to overlook its location right next to Quattro Cantini and the fact that it's cheaper than nearby hotels.

In conclusion, I feel like I had a good experience overall.

reviews.title: Great location

Our room was very basic - by the time we booked only 'standard' rooms were available.  Having said that - we were comfortable and didn't spend much time in the room.

reviews.text: La ubicación del hotel no puede ser mejor. Sin embargo las habitaciones están viejas, los baños necesitan una mejoría y el desayuno no es muy contundente, sobre todo en fruta, que no pusieron casi nada.

reviews.title: The location is really good.
reviews.text: The location is really good, right next to Quattro Plaza. The path from the station to the accommodation is a bit shabby, but the streets get cleaner as you get closer to the accommodation. There are many nice restaurants and bars in the nearby alley in the evening. The room is spacious and nice, but it doesn’t get much light and the lighting is a bit dim, which was a bit uncomfortable.

reviews.title: Would stay here again in the warmer months, but skip the breakfast, the location is hard to beat.
reviews.positives:
The location is very good - perfect for a walking visit to the old part of Palermo.
The staff is pleasant and helpful.
reviews.negatives: The rooms were very cold - around 13-14 deg C. Heating supposedly starts working Dec 1st (our stay was last week of November). Unacceptable for a hotel. The coffee is subpar and the breakfast is very "Italian" (heavy on sweets, little to no choice of other things).

reviews.title: Budget room with perfect location & quality breakfast
reviews.text:
We chose a discounted price room, with no guaranteeed layout (triple / double with single , double with couch). We quickly found out, why it was discounted, it was quite a dark room with a panorama to the inner yard with a huge ventillation pipe providing air for the guest rooms and constant noise for us in  0-24. Fortunately we are well-prepared travellers, room was needed only for sleeping, therefore an earplug solved the problem quickly. Agoda app stated we paid everything included taxes, however we were charged with city tax fee at arrival - be aware of this. Still waiting for customer service feedback on this issue.

Hotel has perfect location, in the center of everything, minimarket within 10m, main street with restaurant and bars on the corner, port is at the end of the street. Breakfast was high quality including items you are not expecting even for 4 star hotels, with an outstanding heart-warming panorama on the roof. Having your coffee outside is recommended, even in chillier weather!

reviews.title: Very basic accommodation steps from Quattro Canti
reviews.positives: Location could not be better. Rooftop bar views were spectacular, appreciated the 10% off first drink. Parking was close.
reviews.negatives: The room was not clean - there was a significant amount of mold in the bathroom, especially in and around the shower. The lobby floor had a very strong fragrance which seemed to try and cover up sewage smells. The parking situation was quite intricate and definitely a deterrent not to use the car while in Palermo - would advise to only use the nearby garage if you have a very small car.

reviews.title: Amazing
reviews.text: The location was absolutely perfect for us as we only had 2 nights in Palermo and wanted to be close to the city centre. The staff was super nice and helpful, the room we had was quite spacious with the view of the atrium inside the building (which we didn’t mind). However, if you’re a light sleeper you may want to pack a set of earplugs as the walls are rather thin and can hear pretty much any noise.

reviews.title: Great location, not great sleep or showers.
reviews.positives: The breakfast and rooftop terrace were great.  The location within Palermo was excellent.
reviews.negatives: The room was not as clean as I would have liked it.  The bed was uncomfortable- way too firm, sheets were rough and scratchy, and flat pillows. We asked for more pillows which helped some, but it was still very uncomfortable. The shower was mildewed and very small.  The overall look was very minimal and sad.

reviews.title: very good location

reviews.title: Great place for Palermo
reviews.positives: Excellent location for old Town. Modern spacious rooms, well equipped. Friendly helpful staff spoke English.  Incredible two rooftop lounges with excellent food and drinks at reasonable local prices. Comfy beds. Taxi across the street. Sights and shopping right outside.
reviews.negatives: Shower died on me early morning of departure.

reviews.title: The location is good.
reviews.text: The location is really good. Wherever you go, you have to pass by, so you can leave your shopping in the middle, use the restroom, and if you’re tired, you can take a break in the lobby, which is nice. There are delicious Thai restaurants nearby, and since it's close to tourist spots, there are quite a few good restaurants. You can have breakfast on the rooftop on the 5th floor, and the view is nice, so it's worth eating there at least once.

reviews.title: Above average

reviews.title: You get what you pay for

reviews.title: Location, location, location!
reviews.text: Its location just cannot be beaten. B&B Hotel Palermo Quattro Canti is in the very heart of Palermo, just steps away from Quattro Canti.  I could walk to all major sights easily within 10-15 mins. I stayed in a clean and mordern room with smart TV and  was able to watch YouTube videos. The sumptuous buffet breakfast on the rooftop restaurant overlooking domes and towers nearby is really the highlight! The only issue that I had during my stay was the extremely unstable water temperature in the shower.

reviews.title: Great location,  could improve the breakfast & service on such a wonderful panoramic terrace.

reviews.negatives:

reviews.negatives: room was under par, no light, small window looking directly onto the neighbor’s a/c unit 3 feet away, furniture sparse, no night stands, only a tiny shelf on the headboard.

reviews.title: A hotel in a good location in Palermo.
reviews.text: It is located right next to Quattro Canti, making it a good spot to visit restaurants and souvenir shops. Since you'll have to head towards Quattro Canti for meals anyway, I recommend finding accommodations near Quattro Canti rather than the train station. It's not far to walk to Palermo Central Station. The room was just an average class, but it was wider than any hotel I've stayed in. It was fairly clean and offered breakfast, so overall, I am satisfied.

reviews.title: Great!
reviews.text: Comfortable, tidy, had all essentials. Very close to restaurants and public transport. Lovely rooftop. Lovely staff.

reviews.title: An amazing accommodation.
reviews.text: The most important and famous B&B in Palermo is located just 50 meters from Quattre Can't, right at the fourth intersection. Just a few steps away, you can enjoy buskers singing 'O Sole Mio,' and there's plenty to eat and see around the intersection, making it a fantastic place to truly enjoy Palermo. Above all, the three reception staff members greeted us warmly and were incredibly kind! They were friendly, professional, and impressive, providing a level of satisfaction even better than a five-star hotel. The winter heating was excellent, and it was never cold. If I come again, I definitely want to stay here. Thank you.

reviews.title: enjoyable stay in the heart of the city
reviews.text: My stay at this hotel was truly delightful. Situated conveniently near both the train station and the city center, it offered unparalleled accessibility to key attractions. The room was comfortable, and the amenities provided exceeded my expectations. What truly stood out was the exceptional service provided by the staff. From check-in to check-out, they were incredibly helpful and accommodating, ensuring a smooth and enjoyable experience throughout my stay. I highly commend their professionalism and dedication to guest satisfaction. If you're looking for a hassle-free and enjoyable stay in the heart of the city, I highly recommend this hotel.

reviews.title: Excellent Hotel in great location
reviews.text: This hotel was so great. It was in an excellent location, the rooms were spacious, clean, and very nice.  It was pretty quiet in the area, but just steps from all there is to see in Ortigia.  The front desk staff (all of them) were super helpful and friendly.  I would definitely recommend this hotel and Siracusa to anyone who asks!

reviews.title: Nice and Great location
reviews.text:
I stayed two nights at this hotel. The hotel is in the old part of the city and where all the tourist attractions are. So, when guests walk out of the hotel, it is all happening there. There are a lot of shops and restaurants on the streets.
 
The check-in was nice and smooth when I arrived. The staff was friendly. 
 
Of course, I booked a double bed. The room was clean and spacious with a big bathroom, but I don’t like a bathroom with a tub. Anyway… 
 
Breakfast was provided on the top floor of the hotel and there was so much food and a lot of choices.
 
Overall:  B&B Hotel Palermo Quatti was excellent. I definitely recommend friends who want to visit Palermo to stay there.

The male had a couch bed that wasn’t comfortable and we were trying to avoid. The Air conditioning was turned off for winter so extremely HOTin room. If you open window it was extremely LOUD! We arrived back to rooms at 3pm. The maid was “cleaning” one of our two rooms,so all5 of us went to other room. In less than 5 minutes of collapsing in second room,she knocks on door and we said we’d be 5 minutes. She said NO. She wouldn’t give us 5 minutes to gather our things and reconvene in other room. The day before,I thought my husband had thrown the covers up over the bed and thought it was odd. Nope. That’s how the maid did the bed. Just throwing covers over pillow. It’s a shame because this could have been awesome if they had AC and better,nicer cleaning crew. And give us the type room we booked

reviews.title: Great Stay in Palermo – with Parking Tip
reviews.text:
The hotel in Palermo was convincing all around: its central location near the Quattro Canti is ideal for sightseeing. The only downside was arriving by car – this can be a bit tricky due to the restricted traffic zone (ZTL). I recommend parking at Parcheggio Orlando Apcoa, which is about 1.2 km from the hotel (roughly a 15-minute walk).

You can stop briefly in front of the hotel to load and unload luggage, but you’ll need a ZTL pass for access. This pass can be purchased online via the PalerMobilità app. Currently, the pass is valid only until 6 a.m. the following morning and costs €5.

Other than that, the stay was fantastic: clean rooms, friendly staff, beautiful views, and a pleasant, walkable city center. We slept well and enjoyed the warm water. A thoroughly enjoyable stay!

reviews.title: The hotel is nice and comfortable, area is noisy and busy, up to you what you make of it.
reviews.positives:
The staff was excellent, very friendly and helpful. Most of the staff spoke English .They arraigned transportation to and from the hotel and made reservations for us at the rooftop restaurant. The rooftop restaurant has a great view of Palermo and the food and drinks was very good, it was perfect for our first night in Palermo after a long flight from NY.
     The room was comfortable and clean. It had a large window overlooking the street with a nice view. The shower had good pressure and plenty of hot water. 
      The hotel is located steps away from the Quattro Canti and walking distance to most attractions. It is ideally located in the center of Palermo, close to everything.
reviews.negatives: It can be very noisy, the street outside our room was busy until at least 2:00 am. On our second night the music from the Quattro Canti and the crowds of people woke us up at 12:30 am. We were annoyed that we couldn’t sleep but decided to go downstairs, have a drink and join the crowd. It turned out to be a lot of fun and. Great night.

Also, I had to exit with my car before opening time and the parking staff prepared everything so I could exit autonomously.
reviews.negatives:
Our room was one of the three in a mezzanine floor, in a different section of the building still accessible from the inside with a few steps through what seemed an emergency exit. 
Parking doesn't belong to the hotel, when we arrived the first day, it was full even if I contacted the hotel in advance confirming I needed a slot. I had to park in a parking nearby for one night, much worse than the Garage San Matteo which we could use for the other days.

reviews.positives: Our stay at the hotel was almost perfect. It was beautiful, with an amazing location! The room was spacious, and the bed was great. The staff was generally friendly and super helpful.
reviews.negatives:
There was however once incident that marked our experience:

As we were checking out, my 2-year-old child wandered close to a railing in the lobby. A receptionist promptly warned us, and I quickly picked up my child. Immediately after, I overheard him receptionist speaking to a colleague in Italian, making negative remarks about parents behavior, thinking I couldn't understand. 

I let him know that I understood Italian. He came over more than once to apologize and insisted that they were discussing something else, though I remain doubtful. 

While I appreciate his multiple apologies, I believe such comments are unprofessional and should not be made, regardless of whether guests can understand the language. 

Overall, I still think highly of the hotel and would recommend it, but this incident was a disappointing end to an otherwise wonderful stay.

reviews.title: Fantastic hotel weird room
reviews.positives: Location is superb. No walking needed, it's right in the middle of it all. Cabs always parked outside as well it's that central. It was impeccably clean and house keeping was like clockwork. The bed and pillows were memory foam and great. Room was a decent size too as was the bathroom. Looked and felt like a proper hotel, not like a DIY or "like home" feel that so many hotels in Europe now push on you (more work for you, cheaper for them and honestly I stopped enjoying that experience a year after it caught steam... Give me a real hotel any time now!). Very good breakfast considering b&b breakfast mostly in Italy is packaged sweet stuff. A huge variety of baked goods, sweet and savory, coffees, egg, beads, croissants, fruit, cheese etc.
reviews.negatives: The hotel was like a cave. Almost zero natural light in my room. Labyrinthine layout that goes inwards instead of sideways so very few or no windows. The room, although large, had the most bizarre layout- the bed got in the way of everything! The bathroom door only opened half way because of it and at one  place where the tv was mounted on the wall, I had to tilt myself sideways to pass (right in front of the main door too). If I had more than 2 drinks and came back to that layout, I would've knocked the tv down and broken my shoulder too. There was a spot that blasted music somewhere and I had to keep closing the emergency exit doors that carried the music downstairs. I'm not sure why they were kept open each day. So overall, I loved it but next time I would make sure not to get room 128 if I wanted to not contort my way around the room.

reviews.title: Necessita de melhorias!
reviews.text:
O hotel fica dentro da Zona de Tráfego Limitado, o que significa um custo adicional de 2,50 Euros/dia para quem vai de carro. O estacionamento (15 Euros/noite) fica um pouco afastado.
O hotel tem uma lavanderia, mas a máquina de secar não estava secando, e não havia qualquer aviso. Perdemos 4 Euros e ainda tivemos que levar as roupas para secar numa lavanderia externa, a 200 m do hotel.
O cofre do quarto não funcionou, falei com a recepcionista, mas nada mudou até o fim da minha estadia.
O café da manhã é cobrado à parte (8 Euros/pessoa), mas as frutas são servidas inteiras e verdes, e das 3 máquinas de café, 2 estavam inoperantes.
O elevador travou várias vezes durante a estadia, forçando-nos a descer pelas escadas.
Os atendentes da recepção são ótimos, mas os demais nem respondem bom dia, bem mal-humorados.
O preço da diária parece bem elevado frente a esses problemas, mas a localização é ótima, a poucos passos da Piazza Pretoria e de Quattro Canti.

reviews.title: Great place to stay in the heart of Palermo; feel the pulse of the city!!

The hotel is literally located in the heart of Palermo, a few steps from the historic Quattro Canti.  If you want to be immersed in the colorful city life of Palermo, this is your place!   So much is just steps away.  Loved it!
reviews.negatives: If peace and quite is important to you, you might not be delighted here.  Plenty of "entertainment" from the Quattro Canti a few steps away can be heard until about 1 am.  Lively is an understatement!  Not a problem for us!
